message.reasoning_content:
We need to produce JSON for stage web-002-backlink: create webpage 2 and link it back to page 1 without breaking page 1. Existing generated files: site/index.html (page 1). We need to create site/page-2.html that links back to page 1 (index.html). Also we must not break page 1, so we should not modify index.html. So only new file: site/page-2.html. The page-2.html should have a link to index.html (maybe Back to Home) and possibly some content. Also need to ensure it's valid HTML. We need to output JSON with files array containing that file. Also notes. Make sure to use relative paths from run root: "site/page-2.html". Content: a full HTML document. Let's craft: Page 2
This is page 2. Back to Home
We could also include a nav that will grow later but not needed. Now produce JSON. Check that we don't include any extra text. Thus final answer: { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-2.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-2.html with a link back to index.html." } Make sure JSON is valid: escape quotes inside content? In JSON string we need to escape double quotes. The content includes double quotes around attribute values. We need to escape them with backslash. Also need to escape any existing backslashes? Not needed. Thus we need to produce a string where double quotes are escaped. Let's construct content string: "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" We need to escape the double quotes inside: each " becomes \". Thus: "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" But careful: we need to escape for JSON: the outer string uses double quotes, so any internal double quote must be preceded by a backslash. So we replace each " with \". Thus the content value becomes: "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" Check: we have backslash before each double quote. That's correct. Now produce JSON. Let's write: { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-2.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n
\n This is page 2.\n Back to Home\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-2.html with a link back to index.html." } Make sure no trailing commas. Now output only that JSON.
